args.command: mkdir -p /workspace/research /workspace/decisions && echo "Workspace ready"

cat > /workspace/research/notes.md << 'NOTES'
# Research Notes — Password Manager Evaluation

## Date: 2025-06-18

## Products to Evaluate
1. 1Password (Teams/Business)
2. Bitwarden (Teams/Business)
3. LastPass (Business)
4. Dashlane (Business)
5. Keeper (Business)

## Criteria to Research
- Per-seat pricing (annual)
- SSO integration (Google Workspace, Okta, etc.)
- Audit logging
- SOC 2 / FedRAMP compliance
- Mobile + desktop coverage
- Developer tool integrations (CLI, CI/CD, browser extensions)
- Admin console UX
- Security incident history
- Onboarding/migration tools

## Methodology
- Visit each product's pricing page
- Visit each product's trust/compliance page
- Check GitHub for CLI tools
- Search for security incidents
- Cross-reference with independent reviews
NOTES
echo "Notes initialized"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html "https://1password.com/pricing/" && echo "1Password pricing: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html) bytes"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-trust.html "https://1password.com/trust/" && echo "1Password trust: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-trust.html) bytes"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-features.html "https://1password.com/features/" && echo "1Password features: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-features.html) bytes"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-business.html "https://1password.com/business/" && echo "1Password business: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-business.html) bytes"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-cli.html "https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/" && echo "1Password CLI: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-cli.html) bytes"
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/1password-secrets.html "https://1password.com/products/secrets-manager/" && echo "1Password Secrets: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/1password-secrets.html) bytes"
grep -i -E 'price|per user|per seat|month|year|annual|business|teams|\$' /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html | head -60

# Try to get pricing from the page text
strings /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+.*per|per.*user|per.*month|per.*seat|business|teams|enterprise' | head -30

# Extract 1Password pricing clearly
echo "=== 1Password Pricing (from pricing page) ==="
strings /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html | grep -E 'priceAnnual|priceMonthly|postPriceDetails' | grep -i 'business\|teams\|starter' | head -20
echo ""
echo "=== 1Password Business USD ==="
strings /workspace/research/1password-pricing.html | grep -A2 'Business (USD)'

# Bitwarden pricing
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/bitwarden-pricing.html "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/" && echo "Bitwarden pricing: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/bitwarden-pricing.html) bytes"

# Bitwarden trust/compliance
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/bitwarden-trust.html "https://bitwarden.com/trust/" && echo "Bitwarden trust: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/bitwarden-trust.html) bytes"

# Bitwarden compliance page
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/bitwarden-compliance.html "https://bitwarden.com/compliance/" && echo "Bitwarden compliance: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/bitwarden-compliance.html) bytes"

# Bitwarden CLI
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/bitwarden-cli.html "https://bitwarden.com/help/cli/" && echo "Bitwarden CLI: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/bitwarden-cli.html) bytes"

# Extract Bitwarden pricing
strings /workspace/research/bitwarden-pricing.html | grep -i -E 'price|per user|per month|annual|business|teams|enterprise|\$[0-9]' | head -40

# Get Bitwarden business pricing page
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/bitwarden-business-pricing.html "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/business/" && echo "Bitwarden business pricing: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/bitwarden-business-pricing.html) bytes"

# Extract Bitwarden pricing data
strings /workspace/research/bitwarden-business-pricing.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+.*per|per.*user|per.*month|annual|teams|business' | head -30

# LastPass pricing
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing.html "https://www.lastpass.com/pricing" && echo "LastPass pricing: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing.html) bytes"

# Dashlane pricing
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" && echo "Dashlane pricing: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html) bytes"

# Keeper pricing

# Get LastPass pricing data
strings /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+.*per|per.*user|per.*month|annual|business|teams|enterprise' | head -20

# Try LastPass business pricing page
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/lastpass-business.html "https://www.lastpass.com/products/business" && echo "LastPass business: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/lastpass-business.html) bytes"

# Extract LastPass pricing
strings /workspace/research/lastpass-business.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+.*per|per.*user|per.*month|annual|business|premium|essential|advanced' | head -20

# Try LastPass pricing page with different URL
curl -sL -o /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html "https://www.lastpass.com/select-business-plan" && echo "LastPass select plan: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html) bytes"

# Extract LastPass pricing from select plan page
strings /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+|per.*user|per.*month|annual|premium|essential|advanced|business' | head -30

# LastPass pricing is dynamic - let's check their API or look for pricing data

curl -sL -o /workspace/research/lastpass-compare.html "https://www.lastpass.com/products/business/compare" && echo "LastPass compare: $(wc -c < /workspace/research/lastpass-compare.html) bytes"

# Check LastPass pricing from their API or JS
strings /workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html | grep -i -E 'LPBusiness|price|amount|\$' | head -20

# LastPass pricing is loaded dynamically. Let's try their API endpoint
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/api/v1/pricing?lang=en" 2>/dev/null | head -200
echo "---"
# Also try the pricing API
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/api/pricing" 2>/dev/null | head -200

# Let me use Python to parse the JS-rendered pages and also get Dashlane/Keeper pricing
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

# Parse LastPass pricing from the HTML
with open('/workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for pricing data in JS variables
# The pricing is loaded dynamically, let's check for any price data

print("LastPass prices found:", prices[:20])

# Check for plan names

print("LastPass plans found:", set(plans))
PYEOF

# Let me use a Python script to fetch and parse all pricing pages more effectively python3 << 'PYEOF' import urllib.request import re import json # For LastPass, try to find pricing in the JS bundle with open('/workspace/research/lastpass-pricing2.html', 'r') as f: lp = f.read() # Look for script tags with pricing data scripts = re.findall(r']*>(.*?)', lp, re.DOTALL) for i, s in enumerate(scripts): if 'price' in s.lower() or 'amount' in s.lower() or 'LPBusiness' in s: # Print first 500 chars print(f"Script {i}: {s[:500]}") print("---") # Also look for data attributes data_attrs = re.findall(r'data-product="([^"]+)"', lp) print("\nData products:", data_attrs) # Look for pricing in the HTML structure price_sections = re.findall(r'lp-price-tile-cost__amount[^>]*>(.*?)', lp, re.DOTALL) print("\nPrice amounts:", price_sections) PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

# Check the pricing page for any embedded pricing data
with open('/workspace/research/lastpass-pricing-main.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for any JSON data or pricing objects
json_blocks = re.findall(r'\{[^{}]*price[^{}]*\}', content[:50000])
for block in json_blocks[:5]:
    print(block[:200])
    print("---")

# Look for pricing in meta tags
meta_prices = re.findall(r'<meta[^>]*content="[^"]*\$[0-9]+[^"]*"[^>]*>', content)
for m in meta_prices:
    print(m)
PYEOF

# Let me try a different approach - search for LastPass pricing from a cached/alternative source
# Also get Dashlane and Keeper pricing data

# Dashlane pricing
strings /workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html | grep -i -E '\$[0-9]+.*per|per.*user|per.*month|annual|business|teams|premium|advanced' | head -20

# Extract Dashlane pricing from the JSON data
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find the __NEXT_DATA__ JSON
match = re.search(r'<script id="__NEXT_DATA__"[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
if match:
    data = json.loads(match.group(1))
    # Navigate to pricing columns
    blocks = data['props']['pageProps']['__SEED_NODE__']['editorBlocks']
    for block in blocks:
        if block.get('name') == 'tenup/pricing-column':
            attrs = block.get('attributes', {})
            heading = attrs.get('heading', 'N/A')
            custom_price = attrs.get('customPrice', 'N/A')
            per = attrs.get('per', 'N/A')
            above_heading = attrs.get('aboveHeading', 'N/A')
            print(f"Plan: {heading}")
            print(f"  Price: {custom_price}")
            print(f"  Per: {per}")
            print(f"  Above: {above_heading}")
            print()
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html', 'r') as f:

# Find the __NEXT_DATA__ JSON

if match:

    # Navigate to pricing columns

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find the __NEXT_DATA__ JSON
match = re.search(r'<script id="__NEXT_DATA__"[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
if match:
    data = json.loads(match.group(1))
    # Navigate to pricing columns
    page_props = data['props']['pageProps']
    # Check the structure
    print("Top-level keys:", list(page_props.keys()))
    seed = page_props.get('__SEED_NODE__', {})
    print("Seed keys:", list(seed.keys()))
    # Check template query data
    tqd = page_props.get('__TEMPLATE_QUERY_DATA__', {})
    print("TQD keys:", list(tqd.keys()))
    if 'page' in tqd:
        page = tqd['page']
        print("Page keys:", list(page.keys()))
        if 'editorBlocks' in page:
            blocks = page['editorBlocks']
            print(f"Found {len(blocks)} blocks")
            for block in blocks:
                name = block.get('name', 'N/A')
                if 'pricing' in name.lower():
                    attrs = block.get('attributes', {})
                    heading = attrs.get('heading', 'N/A')
                    custom_price = attrs.get('customPrice', 'N/A')
                    per = attrs.get('per', 'N/A')
                    print(f"Block: {name}")
                    print(f"  Heading: {heading}")
                    print(f"  Price: {custom_price}")
                    print(f"  Per: {per}")
                    print()
PYEOF

# Get Dashlane Password Management pricing - it says None but let me check the tabular content
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

match = re.search(r'<script id="__NEXT_DATA__"[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
if match:
    data = json.loads(match.group(1))
    page = data['props']['pageProps']['__TEMPLATE_QUERY_DATA__']['page']
    blocks = page['editorBlocks']
    
    # Find tabular content pricing cells
    for block in blocks:
        name = block.get('name', '')
        if 'tabular' in name.lower() and 'pricing' in name.lower():
            attrs = block.get('attributes', {})
            heading = attrs.get('heading', 'N/A')
            custom_price = attrs.get('customPrice', 'N/A')
            per = attrs.get('per', 'N/A')
            print(f"Tabular: {heading} | Price: {custom_price} | Per: {per}")
    
    # Also check for the Password Management price in the tabular section
    print("\n--- All pricing columns ---")
    for block in blocks:
        name = block.get('name', '')
        if 'pricing-column' in name.lower():
            attrs = block.get('attributes', {})
            heading = attrs.get('heading', 'N/A')
            custom_price = attrs.get('customPrice', 'N/A')
            per = attrs.get('per', 'N/A')
            above = attrs.get('aboveHeading', 'N/A')
            print(f"Column: {heading} | Price: {custom_price} | Per: {per} | Above: {above}")
PYEOF

# Dashlane Password Management price is None in the column but $11 in tabular. Let me check the actual page more carefully
# The tabular content shows Password Management at $11/user/month. The column shows None.
# Let me also check if there's a different price for the Password Management plan
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/dashlane-pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Search for all price mentions
prices = re.findall(r'customPrice["\s:]+["\s]*([\$0-9]+|None|Custom)', content)
print("All customPrice values:", prices)

# Also search for $ signs near "per user"
price_contexts = re.findall(r'.{0,50}\$[0-9]+.{0,50}', content)
for ctx in price_contexts[:10]:
    print(ctx.replace('\n', ' '))
    print("---")
PYEOF

# Now let me get Keeper pricing
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/workspace/research/keeper-pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Search for pricing data
prices = re.findall(r'\$[0-9]+\.?\d*', content)
print("Keeper prices found:", set(prices))

# Search for plan names

print("Keeper plans found:", set(plans))

# Look for per-user pricing

for ctx in per_user[:5]:
    print(ctx.replace('\n', ' '))
    print("---")
